## What Changed

- Canonicalized the MCP `tb_*` tool catalog into the shared contracts layer instead of keeping the schema definitions only inside the bridge implementation
- Generated and shipped MCP contract artifacts for both JSON and module consumers so the bridge, validation tests, and standalone bundle all read the same catalog source
- Added versioned persistence for search profile metadata so installed runtimes can evolve `preferred-engine.json` and `*.profile-state.json` more safely
- Reduced CLI entry complexity without changing behavior to satisfy the Sonar maintainability gate
- Fixed the quality gate regression by excluding generated MCP contract artifacts from Biome formatting checks

## User Impact

- Installed MCP usage from `touch-browser mcp` remains the primary local flow introduced in `v0.1.4`
- MCP tool definitions are now backed by a canonical shared contract, which lowers drift risk between the bridge, docs, tests, and bundled runtime
- Existing search metadata files continue to load, and new writes include explicit version information for future migrations
- No new user-facing command was added in this release; this version focuses on contract stability and release quality

## Notes

- This release follows `v0.1.4` and keeps the same release-first install/update/uninstall workflow
- The main code change after the failed Sonar run was a CI stability fix only: generated MCP artifacts are now treated as generated outputs, not hand-formatted source files
